demo-app / my-next-project

Critical risk
https://my-next-project.vercel.appScanned May 27, 2026 at 3:42 PM

Critical
0
High

Medium

5 vulnerabilities found

criticalExposed Secret
src/lib/supabase.js:14

Supabase service role key hardcoded in client bundle. This key bypasses Row Level Security and grants full database access.

const supabase = createClient(url, 'eyJh...')
Fix prompt — paste into Lovable / Cursor

Remove the hardcoded Supabase service role key from src/lib/supabase.js. Move it to an environment variable called SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and access it via process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY on the server only. Use the anon key for client-side Supabase calls instead.

highMissing Authentication
app/api/admin/users/route.ts:3

Admin API route has no authentication check. Any unauthenticated request can list and delete all users.

export async function GET() {
  const users = await db.users.findAll()
  return Response.json(users)
}
Fix prompt — paste into Lovable / Cursor

Add authentication to app/api/admin/users/route.ts. Import auth from @clerk/nextjs/server and call const { userId } = auth() at the top of your handler. Return a 401 if userId is null. Add admin role checking before returning sensitive data.

highSQL Injection
lib/db.ts:47

Raw SQL query built with string concatenation allows SQL injection attacks.

const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '${email}'`
Fix prompt — paste into Lovable / Cursor

Replace the string-concatenated SQL query in lib/db.ts line 47 with a parameterised query. Use db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = $1", [email]) instead. Never interpolate user input directly into SQL strings.

mediumOpen CORS
app/api/data/route.ts:8

Access-Control-Allow-Origin set to wildcard (*) allows any origin to call this API.

headers.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*')
Fix prompt — paste into Lovable / Cursor

Replace the wildcard CORS header in app/api/data/route.ts with your specific allowed origin. Change headers.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*') to headers.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', 'https://yourdomain.com'). For multiple allowed origins, implement origin checking logic.

mediumVulnerable Dependency
package.json:23

lodash@4.17.15 has a known prototype pollution vulnerability (CVE-2021-23337).

"lodash": "4.17.15"
Fix prompt — paste into Lovable / Cursor

Update lodash to the latest version in package.json. Run: npm install lodash@latest. Then run npm audit to check for any remaining vulnerabilities. Consider replacing lodash utility functions with native JavaScript equivalents to reduce dependency surface.
